title: Fresh Prince After Dark

jokes.text: They're gonna call it 'Warm Fronts.'
jokes.setup: Playboy pictorial on prettiest weather girls
jokes.punchline: Double entendre title combining weather terminology with sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - weather terminology with clear sexual double meaning. Economical and well-constructed.

jokes.text: You heard our father: No! Carlton, what do you care?
jokes.setup: Carlton immediately supports father's decision against Hilary posing
jokes.punchline: Will points out Carlton's hypocritical investment in his sister's modeling
jokes.explanation: Solid character-based humor highlighting Carlton's uptight nature and Will's street-smart observations.

jokes.text: Now, now, don't get your Underoos in a knot, stumpy.
jokes.setup: Carlton getting upset about family scandal
jokes.punchline: Will mocks him with childish underwear reference and height insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will Smith delivery combining brand name humor with physical comedy reference to Carlton's height. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Long afternoons kicking back on a chaise longue... watching full-body contact volleyball up at Hef's place.
jokes.setup: Will supporting Hilary's Playboy decision
jokes.punchline: Reveals his selfish motivation - access to Playboy Mansion activities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's teenage male perspective and self-interest behind his support.

jokes.text: That's telling us, Dad.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil laying down the law to everyone
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic response undermining Phil's authority
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective sarcasm delivered with Will's characteristic irreverence toward authority.

jokes.text: What were we talking about?
jokes.setup: Hilary trying to make passionate speech about her independence
jokes.punchline: She loses her train of thought mid-argument
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - her airheaded nature undermining her attempt at serious adult discourse. Great character comedy.

jokes.text: Hil, I think you were in the process of storming out of here.
jokes.setup: Hilary's confused state after losing her train of thought
jokes.punchline: Will helpfully reminds her what dramatic gesture she was attempting
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to the awkward moment with Will's helpful deadpan delivery. Shows his role as family mediator/commentator.

jokes.text: Look like you got hat-jacked by Woody Woodpecker.
jokes.setup: Will seeing someone wearing a ridiculous hat
jokes.punchline: Comparing the hat to cartoon character Woody Woodpecker
jokes.explanation: Creative visual comparison with street slang ('hat-jacked'). Very quotable line with good imagery.

jokes.text: Has it changed since we used to play doctor?
jokes.setup: Jackie saying she doesn't want people looking at her body
jokes.punchline: Will's inappropriate reference to childhood sexual exploration
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor playing on Will's inappropriate flirtation style. Gets laugh from discomfort.

jokes.text: Will, you just hit on me from force of habit.
jokes.setup: Will's automatic flirtation attempts
jokes.punchline: Jackie calling out his unconscious player behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - Jackie recognizing Will's automatic player mode. Smart writing about dating behavior.

jokes.text: 'Okay' is all right if I offered you a government cheese sandwich... but I'm offering you a buffet of love, child.
jokes.setup: Jackie's casual acceptance of his dinner invitation
jokes.punchline: Will's over-the-top metaphor comparing himself to a love buffet
jokes.explanation: Classic Will Smith charm with creative food metaphors. 'Buffet of love' is highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: You know what the evening leads... I'm sorry, I need to have that treated.
jokes.setup: Will starting another inappropriate sexual comment
jokes.punchline: He catches himself and acknowledges his compulsive behavior needs help
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware humor where Will recognizes his own problematic behavior pattern. Shows character growth potential.

jokes.text: I know. I look great naked.
jokes.setup: Hilary giving body acceptance speech about teenage awkwardness
jokes.punchline: Ashley reveals her confidence isn't the issue - she just doesn't like being stared at
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - subverts expectation of teenage insecurity with confident self-assessment. Great economy.

jokes.text: I guess I could kiss heaven goodbye... because it got to be a sin to look this good.
jokes.setup: Will admiring himself in the mirror
jokes.punchline: Religious metaphor about his good looks being sinful
jokes.explanation: Classic Will Smith vanity humor. Character-specific but not particularly original or surprising.

jokes.text: You've been playing tennis with the gardener again, haven't you?
jokes.setup: Will's confident statement about looking good
jokes.punchline: Hilary implies he's been having affair with gardener
jokes.explanation: Innuendo about Will's appearance coming from romantic activity. Moderately clever but not outstanding.

jokes.text: I think you're still in the process of forgetting.
jokes.setup: Hilary asking if she forgot to tell Will about something
jokes.punchline: Will's clever wordplay about her ongoing forgetfulness
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that captures Hilary's perpetual absent-mindedness. Good structure and economy.

jokes.text: Let me tell you, you're the best cousin a brother ever had.
jokes.setup: Will's excitement about going to Playboy Mansion
jokes.punchline: Mixing family relationship terms in his enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's excitement making him mix up relationship terminology. Character-specific but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Well, hello, Hef. Oh, pshaw. Don't be silly. I'm glad I could come. Oh, Miss December. Oh, baby, I was gonna call you.
jokes.setup: Will getting ready for the party
jokes.punchline: He's practicing conversations with imaginary Playboy personalities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's teenage fantasies and preparation. Physical comedy of him talking to himself.

jokes.text: if you can tear yourself away from your imaginary friend
jokes.setup: Geoffrey observing Will's fantasy rehearsal
jokes.punchline: Dryly calling out Will's behavior as talking to imaginary friends
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - dry British wit observing American teenager behavior. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Tell me, might this young lady have a terminal illness?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey noting that Will really cares about Jackie
jokes.punchline: Suggests only terminal illness would justify missing Playboy Mansion
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor delivered with perfect British deadpan. Subversive take on romantic priorities vs. teenage male desires.

jokes.text: Then may I point out, she'll still be here tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Confirming Jackie doesn't have terminal illness
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's practical advice prioritizing once-in-a-lifetime opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up to the terminal illness joke. Geoffrey's dry logic about prioritizing rare opportunities.

jokes.text: Oh, they don't need you, G. They're fully staffed.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asking when they leave for the Mansion
jokes.punchline: Will crushes Geoffrey's hopes of joining them
jokes.explanation: Creates awkward moment as Will obliviously disappoints Geoffrey. Minor cringe humor.

jokes.text: Either that is one tight dress or a really good paint job.
jokes.setup: Hilary appearing in extremely form-fitting outfit
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting the dress is so tight it might be painted on
jokes.explanation: Standard visual gag about tight clothing. Competent but not particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: No, no, no, it's only the rest of his life. After them pictures come out, how much longer could he have?
jokes.setup: Hilary worried about father hating her for rest of his life
jokes.punchline: Will suggests the shock might kill Uncle Phil
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalating the family conflict. Shows Will's tendency to make situations worse with inappropriate comments.

jokes.text: He's got issues in his bathroom from, like, 1964.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Uncle Phil not liking Playboy
jokes.punchline: Reveals Phil's hypocrisy - he has vintage Playboy collection
jokes.explanation: Excellent hypocrisy reveal that pays off Phil's earlier moral stance. Great callback with perfect timing.

jokes.text: Oh, look, the Little Republican Store must've opened.
jokes.setup: Carlton appearing dressed up
jokes.punchline: Will mocking Carlton's conservative appearance and politics
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-based insult capturing Carlton's conservative nature through retail metaphor. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I'm not gonna miss out on Princeton... because she wants to expose her weather balloons.
jokes.setup: Carlton explaining why he needs to stop Hilary
jokes.punchline: Double entendre comparing breasts to weather balloons, tying back to weather girl theme
jokes.explanation: Clever callback to weather theme with euphemistic language typical of Carlton's awkward attempts at being crude.

jokes.text: Carlton, I think Geoffrey's baking cookies.
jokes.setup: Carlton insisting he must come to the party
jokes.punchline: Will trying to distract Carlton with domestic comfort
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection playing on Carlton's domestic preferences vs. wild parties. Economical but predictable.

jokes.text: Boy gets hot and bothered talking to average-looking girls. Five minutes at the Mansion, he's gonna go into meltdown.
jokes.setup: Hilary asking why Will is confident Carlton won't interfere
jokes.punchline: Will explains Carlton will be overwhelmed by attractive women
jokes.explanation: Excellent character insight about Carlton's inexperience with women. Sets up his later behavior at the Mansion.

jokes.text: Except the girls ain't got folds in the middle and jokes on their back.
jokes.setup: Will comparing real Playboy Mansion to the magazine
jokes.punchline: Reference to magazine's physical characteristics - centerfold and back page
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about the difference between magazine and reality. References physical properties of Playboy magazine.

jokes.text: You're drooling on my pumps.
jokes.setup: Carlton presumably overwhelmed by Mansion environment
jokes.punchline: Physical evidence of his overwhelmed state affecting others
jokes.explanation: Pays off Will's prediction perfectly. Visual gag of Carlton's physical reaction to attractive environment.

jokes.text: Yeah, and your head is like a peanut.
jokes.setup: Carlton saying his legs are like rubber
jokes.punchline: Will's characteristic insult about Carlton's head size
jokes.explanation: Standard Will insult about Carlton's physical appearance. Expected but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I have never seen so many people in one place... I wanted to give mouth-to-mouth resuscitation to.
jokes.setup: Carlton overwhelmed by attractive women at Mansion
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic way of expressing sexual attraction through medical metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton line - awkward attempt at being smooth using medical terminology. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: You who? Stop yodeling. Hugh Hefner.
jokes.setup: Confusion over Will calling to Hugh Hefner
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding creates inadvertent yodeling sound effect
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay confusion creating sound-based humor. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: You def, Hef.
jokes.setup: Will trying to sound cool meeting Hugh Hefner
jokes.punchline: Rhyming slang attempt to connect with Hefner
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's street-smart attempt to connect through wordplay. Character-specific and quotable.

jokes.text: Hey, Carlton, I gotta be honest, man, I forgot you was even here.
jokes.setup: Carlton complaining about not being introduced
jokes.punchline: Will's honest admission he completely forgot about Carlton
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor from Will's thoughtless honesty. Creates awkward moment highlighting Carlton's invisibility.

jokes.text: They think I'm the black dude from Mod Squad.
jokes.setup: Jazz appearing at exclusive Playboy party
jokes.punchline: He got in through mistaken identity with 1970s TV show actor
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jazz moment - his usual scheme involving pop culture reference. Perfect character consistency and very quotable.

jokes.text: Too bad. They're not here.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asking if Uncle Phil would like dinner with the children
jokes.punchline: Classic Geoffrey fake-out after building false expectation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - sets up false hope then delivers deadpan disappointment. Excellent structure and character integration.

jokes.text: If you don't care where your children are, sir, neither do I.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asking why Geoffrey brought up dinner if kids aren't there
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's passive-aggressive response about Phil's parenting
jokes.explanation: Excellent Geoffrey sass with subtle critique of Phil's parenting. Perfect character moment with underlying message.

jokes.text: That one's on the house.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey revealing children's location after claiming he wouldn't betray confidence
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging he gave away information for free
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-commentary on Geoffrey's own behavior. Self-aware humor about his sass delivery.

jokes.text: How come you knew that and I didn't? Because they talk to me. Why don't they talk to me? Because you don't listen.
jokes.setup: Phil discovering he's out of loop on family activities
jokes.punchline: Vivian's blunt diagnosis of Phil's communication problems
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamics humor highlighting Phil's authoritarian but disconnected parenting style.

jokes.text: Looks like everything on that body is in working order.
jokes.setup: Woman mentioning she'll be in surgery
jokes.punchline: Will's inappropriate medical double entendre
jokes.explanation: Standard inappropriate pickup line playing on medical terminology. Predictable and not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I'm a cardiologist. You need a pediatrician.
jokes.setup: Will's inappropriate advance on doctor
jokes.punchline: She insults his maturity level through medical specialization
jokes.explanation: Brilliant comeback using medical expertise to deliver devastating insult about Will's immaturity. Perfect economy and structure.

jokes.text: Very impressive. Now, do you remember my turnoffs? Hairy backs, smokers... and abrasive young men who don't know when to shut up. Bingo.
jokes.setup: Will showing off by reciting model's turn-ons from magazine
jokes.punchline: She lists turnoffs that perfectly describe Will, ending with 'Bingo'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating putdown with perfect 'Bingo' punchline. Shows Will's pickup strategies backfiring.

jokes.text: Five years and six figures.
jokes.setup: Will asking what it would take to get next to her
jokes.punchline: Specific requirements highlighting impossibility for teenage Will
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in defining exactly what Will lacks. Brutal honesty delivered with deadpan precision.

jokes.text: I've got two numbers... and if I play my cards right, I think she'll give me the other five.
jokes.setup: Carlton bragging about his success with women
jokes.punchline: Reveals he only has partial phone number, needs five more digits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - his inexperience showing through misunderstanding of dating success. Excellent misdirection and character integration.

jokes.text: Bunny hop! / What do you think you're doing? / The bunny hop.
jokes.setup: Will about to have realization about Jackie
jokes.punchline: Carlton interrupting with innocent dance at inappropriate time and place
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Carlton's innocent dancing at the Playboy Mansion. Phil's horrified reaction adds to the humor.

jokes.text: My man, Linc.
jokes.setup: Phil trying to connect with Hugh Hefner
jokes.punchline: References Mod Squad character, callback to Jazz's earlier line
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to earlier Mod Squad joke, showing Phil trying to be hip. Works better on rewatch.

jokes.text: You know, actually, I had an allergic reaction... to bunnies.
jokes.setup: Will explaining his fake illness to Jackie
jokes.punchline: Creates excuse referencing Playboy bunnies
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting his lie to Playboy context. Shows Will's quick thinking but also his dishonesty.

jokes.text: What kind of monster are you?
jokes.setup: Learning Jackie's date is with a large man with mustache
jokes.punchline: Will's dramatic overreaction to normal dating scenario
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's jealousy and dramatic tendencies. Character-appropriate overreaction to competition.

jokes.text: Cinco de Mayo? Rosh Hashanah?
jokes.setup: Will desperately trying to find available date with Jackie
jokes.punchline: Escalates to obscure holidays showing his desperation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Will's desperation through increasingly random holiday suggestions.

jokes.text: In that case, I never would've married you.
jokes.setup: Phil saying daughters should obey fathers
jokes.punchline: Vivian revealing she disobeyed her father by marrying Phil
jokes.explanation: Nice reversal showing the logical flaw in Phil's position through personal example. Good marital banter.

jokes.text: It was a tie between your family and your temper.
jokes.setup: Phil asking what Vivian's father didn't like about him
jokes.punchline: Two specific character flaws that still apply
jokes.explanation: Good character insight delivered as marital roast. Shows ongoing family dynamics and Phil's unchanged nature.

jokes.text: Look at that. She's covered up!
jokes.setup: Phil reluctantly looking at Hilary's Playboy pictures
jokes.punchline: Relief that the photos are tasteful weather shots, not nude
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Phil's anxiety throughout episode. His relief is both touching and funny.

jokes.text: But this little girl is not.
jokes.setup: Phil's relief about Hilary's covered photos
jokes.punchline: Discovers adjacent nude photo of different model
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and structure. Sets up relief then immediately undercuts it with new concern.

jokes.text: Look, I will not be publicly embarrassed... by a problem of delivering my damn lines. It won't happen, damn it. It ain't gonna happen.
jokes.setup: Outtake of Carlton breaking character during scene
jokes.punchline: Actor's frustrated outburst about his performance problems
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor showing actor breaking during the very line about not being embarrassed. Perfect irony and self-referential comedy.

tmdb_overview: Will breaks a date with Jackie to accompany Hilary to a Playboy Mansion reception for weather reporters being photographed for the magazine.
